evidence_quotes: single games or games which one could simple play together via LAN or via a VPN like Diablo 2, Age of Empires 2 or other such games show positively, which is missing in many modern games!

More and more the big publishers narrow what can be developed by smaller firms and get to license simplest game mechanics like catching something, how to fly, playing mini-games while loading a game or any other such basic mechanics.
Such BASIC mechanics shouldnt be able to be licenses and hold others over their head!
Not everything needs to be a license or a service, nevertheless, if those are a license/service/ownership/ those need to be labeled and defined as such!
If you buy a license/service, these need to be defined at least until a specific date or if this license is a lifetime, one needs to be defined as owning such!
On this matter of owning a game, I want to still be able to continue use games, which I own, to be able to use, even if the company doesnt exist anymore, or stops selling that game.

paragraphs.text: While I totally agree with all the agendas here, I would like to include the following as well: The whole principle (censorship) is wrong; it's like demanding that grown men live on skim milk because the baby can't eat steak. Robert A. Heinlein I, as an adult, want to have choices for getting adult content. One doesnt have to use it, but thats that, however, censoring content and stifling this freedom of choice is not good for all of us. Being regulated via the payment systems (Master Card/Visa/PayPal/) is wrong and limits us even from outside of the EU on what we can pay! The parents are responsible to teach and choose which games they provide, give and allow their children to play. Furthermore, I dont like that we own less and less a game, also less and less we can, like board games borrow out our games to friends, which if like will buy their own. Id like to include here that we solidify that what we buy that we own. Not everything needs to be a license or a service, nevertheless, if those are a license/service/ownership/ those need to be labeled and defined as such! If you buy a license/service, these need to be defined at least until a specific date or if this license is a lifetime, one needs to be defined as owning such! On this matter of owning a game, I want to still be able to continue use games, which I own, to be able to use, even if the company doesnt exist anymore, or stops selling that game. I dont mean the MMOs or other multiple online games, but single games or games which one could simple play together via LAN or via a VPN like Diablo 2, Age of Empires 2 or other such games show positively, which is missing in many modern games! Licensing is still from the 80ies, Licensing would have to be actualized. More and more the big publishers narrow what can be developed by smaller firms and get to license simplest game mechanics like catching something, how to fly, playing mini-games while loading a game or any other such basic mechanics. Such BASIC mechanics shouldnt be able to be licenses and hold others over their head! For example in real live it would be like licensing lassoing an object and need to pay whenever someone lassos something
